message:
fix: pool lock scope, waiter FIFO ordering, and snapshot size estimation

Three correctness fixes in pool and distributed subsystems:

1. database/pool.rs — Narrow mutex lock scope in return_connection and
   discard_connection so that manager.disconnect() is called after
   releasing the pool lock.  Previously the disconnect call happened
   inside the critical section, risking deadlock if the manager's
   disconnect implementation attempted to re-acquire the pool.

2. sync/pool.rs — Fix waiter-cleanup race in the generic resource pool.
   The previous code removed the waiter from the queue inside the
   idle-resource path while still holding the lock, which could break
   FIFO ordering when a health check fails and the resource is returned
   to the pool.  The waiter is now only removed at acquire completion
   via remove_waiter, preserving correct ordering.

3. distributed/snapshot.rs — Replace static worst-case byte-size
   estimate for RegionSnapshot serialization with precise conditional
   sizing that checks whether each optional field (deadline_nanos,
   polls_remaining, cost_remaining, cancel_reason, parent) is actually
   present.  The previous estimate over-counted by always including
   space for all optional fields regardless of presence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
